title: Oliver Twist
author: Charles Dickens
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Oliver Twist" book by Charles Dickens?
Options:
A. Master Bates came after, appearing thoughtful. The noise of feet on the creaky steps a couple minutes after this talk took place surprised the happy elderly gentleman as he sat by the fire with a saveloy and tiny loaf in his grasp; a pocketknife in his right hand; and a pewter pot on the trivet. There was a deceitful smile on his white face as he spun around, and gazing keenly out from below his thick red eyebrows, bent his ear toward the door, and listened. 'What's happening here?' grumbled the Jew: altering his face; 'just two of them? Where's the third? They can't be in a mess.'  
B. Master Bates trailed behind, looking pensive. The sound of footsteps on the squeaky stairs a few minutes after this chat happened startled the cheerful old man as he sat by the fire with a saveloy sausage and small loaf in his hand; a pocketknife in his right hand; and a pewter pot on the trivet. There was a wicked grin on his pale face as he turned around, and peering sharply out from under his thick red eyebrows, inclined his ear towards the door, and listened. 'What's going on?' mumbled the Jew: changing expression; 'only two of them? Where's the third? They can't be in trouble.'
C. Master Bates followed, with a thoughtful countenance. The noise of footsteps on the creaking stairs, a few minutes after the occurrence of this conversation, roused the merry old gentleman as he sat over the fire with a saveloy and a small loaf in his hand; a pocket-knife in his right; and a pewter pot on the trivet. There was a rascally smile on his white face as he turned round, and looking sharply out from under his thick red eyebrows, bent his ear towards the door, and listened. 'Why, how's this?' muttered the Jew: changing countenance; 'only two of 'em? Where's the third? They can't have got into trouble.
D. Master Bates followed behind, looking contemplative. The sound of footsteps on the squeaking stairs a few minutes after this exchange startled the cheerful old man as he sat by the fire holding a saveloy and small loaf in one hand, a pocketknife in the other, and with a pewter pot on the trivet. A sly grin spread across his pale face as he turned around, peering sharply out from under his bushy red eyebrows, tilting his ear toward the door to listen. "Now what's all this?" the Jew muttered, his expression changing. "Only two of them? Where's the third? They can't have gotten into trouble."
Answer:

options.A: Master Bates came after, appearing thoughtful. The noise of feet on the creaky steps a couple minutes after this talk took place surprised the happy elderly gentleman as he sat by the fire with a saveloy and tiny loaf in his grasp; a pocketknife in his right hand; and a pewter pot on the trivet. 'What's happening here?' grumbled the Jew: altering his face; 'just two of them? Where's the third? They can't be in a mess.'
options.B: Master Bates trailed behind, looking pensive. The sound of footsteps on the squeaky stairs a few minutes after this chat happened startled the cheerful old man as he sat by the fire with a saveloy sausage and small loaf in his hand; a pocketknife in his right hand; and a pewter pot on the trivet. There was a wicked grin on his pale face as he turned around, and peering sharply out from under his thick red eyebrows, inclined his ear towards the door, and listened. 'What's going on?' mumbled the Jew: changing expression; 'only two of them? Where's the third? They can't be in trouble.'
options.C: Master Bates followed, with a thoughtful countenance. The noise of footsteps on the creaking stairs, a few minutes after the occurrence of this conversation, roused the merry old gentleman as he sat over the fire with a saveloy and a small loaf in his hand; a pocket-knife in his right; and a pewter pot on the trivet. 'Why, how's this?' muttered the Jew: changing countenance; 'only two of 'em? Where's the third? They can't have got into trouble.
options.D: Master Bates followed behind, looking contemplative. The sound of footsteps on the squeaking stairs a few minutes after this exchange startled the cheerful old man as he sat by the fire holding a saveloy and small loaf in one hand, a pocketknife in the other, and with a pewter pot on the trivet.
